Name: spectral_precision_routing
Title: Spectral Precision Routing: Training-Free Optimization via Activation Geometry
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the local covariance condition number and spectral decay rate of hidden states serve as direct proxies for quantization sensitivity, enabling training-free precision allocation and optimization.
Related Work: Our proposal builds upon recent works in spectral quantization routing, geometric interference cancellation, and spectral latent steering. Unlike existing methods, our approach combines activation geometry with PAC-Bayes bounds to provide a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference under natural distribution shifts.
Abstract: We propose Spectral Precision Routing (SPR), a training-free framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on real-time activation geometry. By computing local covariance condition numbers and spectral decay rates of hidden states, we derive a precision-switching policy that enforces strict latency budgets. We theoretically ground SPR in PAC-Bayes bounds, linking spectral conditioning to generalization stability under quantization noise. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across WILDS benchmarks, SPR achieves a superior throughput-accuracy Pareto frontier compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Experiments: Evaluate SPR on WILDS benchmarks, comparing its performance to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines.
Investigate the effect of spectral decay rate and local covariance condition number on quantization sensitivity and precision allocation.
Assess the robustness of SPR to distributional shifts and noisy demonstrations, using adversarial ICL benchmarks and natural domain shifts.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The accuracy of SPR may be affected by the quality of the activation geometry estimates.
The computational overhead of SPR may be higher than that of static mixed-precision baselines.
The theoretical bounds provided by SPR may not hold in practice due to various sources of noise and uncertainty.
